findings.finding: Task 6's subtest matrix does not exercise the staged-only-plugin-tree hand-edit scenario (A seeds via top-level source; no subtest stages an edit only under plugins/cortex-interactive/... with no top-level source).
findings.reviewer_angle: Hook short-circuit correctness on direct plugin-tree hand-edits
findings.evidence_quote: (A) build-output drift (top-level `skills/commit/SKILL.md` edit propagated, plugin-tree diff detected) — asserts hook exits 1.

findings.finding: Existing SKILLS=(commit pr lifecycle ...) at justfile:407 is a recipe-local bash array — the 'readonly bash array' shape Task 3 claims. Task 3 silently departs from this convention without surfacing the structural difference or why cross-recipe sharing requires the different format.
findings.reviewer_angle: Policy-array format: readonly bash arrays vs just-variables inconsistency
findings.evidence_quote: SKILLS=(commit pr lifecycle backlog requirements research discovery refine retro dev fresh diagnose evolve critical-review)

findings.finding: Task 5's mapfile -t BO < <(just _list-build-output-plugins) works only because Task 3 re-materializes the space-joined string as newline-separated output via | tr ' ' '\n'. This contract (helper recipes must emit one-name-per-line) is never documented as load-bearing; Task 3's verification only checks sorted content, not separator.
findings.reviewer_angle: Policy-array format: readonly bash arrays vs just-variables inconsistency
findings.evidence_quote: Load classification via `mapfile -t BO < <(just _list-build-output-plugins)` and `mapfile -t HM < <(just _list-hand-maintained-plugins)`.

findings.finding: Task 6's drift-enforcement tests cover existing classified plugins and an unclassified-directory case, but do not simulate a new build-output plugin directory materializing for the first time. No subtest verifies a newly-created BUILD_OUTPUT plugin directory receives a plugin-appropriate build — the cortex-overnight-integration materialization trap would not be caught by 144's own test suite.
findings.reviewer_angle: Pre-registration of cortex-overnight-integration creates a one-shot directory-guard trap for ticket 121
findings.evidence_quote: Add three subtests (C: hand-maintained edit to plugins/cortex-ui-extras/skills/ui-lint/SKILL.md, D: hand-maintained edit to plugins/cortex-pr-review/skills/pr-review/SKILL.md, E: unclassified plugin dir creation via plugins/cortex-unclassified/.claude-plugin/plugin.json)

findings.finding: Plan's Scope Boundaries correctly notes plugins/cortex-overnight-integration/ is not created by 144, but the adjacent handoff contract to ticket 121 is left misaligned: nothing in Scope Boundaries, Veto Surface, or Follow-ups tells 121 its first materialization commit must atomically introduce either per-plugin SKILLS, HAND_MAINTAINED reclassification, or a pre-flight opt-out.
findings.reviewer_angle: Pre-registration of cortex-overnight-integration creates a one-shot directory-guard trap for ticket 121
findings.evidence_quote: No cortex-overnight-integration plugin directory creation (ticket 121). The name is pre-registered in BUILD_OUTPUT_PLUGINS but no files are created under plugins/cortex-overnight-integration/.

findings.finding: If subtest E's git restore --staged step is missed, re-runs of the test script after a failed run inherit a staged plugins/cortex-unclassified/.claude-plugin/plugin.json in the index. The new Task 5 hook's Phase 1 fail-closed guard will then fire for subtest A's hook invocation, causing A to fail for reasons unrelated to skills drift.
findings.reviewer_angle: Task 6 cleanup semantics: git stash push -u with nonexistent paths and inter-subtest residue
findings.evidence_quote: subtest E must also git add plugins/cortex-unclassified/.claude-plugin/plugin.json before the hook runs — the hook reads staged state, and the fail-closed guard enumerates committed+staged plugin.json files. Unstage in cleanup: git restore --staged plugins/cortex-unclassified/.
